item.title: NAB Tightens Mortgage Borrowing After Property Tax Break Removed
item.description: National Australia Bank Ltd. has tightened its home lending rules after the Australian government removed a perk that allowed property investors to claim mortgage interest payments as a tax deduction.

item.title: Adnoc’s Tankers Are Slipping Oil, Gas and Fuel Through Hormuz
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has been quietly ferrying oil and gas shipments out of the Persian Gulf using its own fleet, apparently circumventing both the Iranian navy and US warships to reach energy-starved customers.

item.title: NTT Docomo Sells Tokyo Land Plots for $371 Million, Joining Boom
item.description: NTT Docomo Inc. sold two plots of land in central Tokyo for about ¥59 billion ($371 million), according to people familiar with the matter, the latest large deal in Japan’s booming real estate market.

item.title: Buyer Says US Allows Ships Sanctioned Over Iran to Be Scrapped
item.description: GMS, a major buyer of ships and offshore vessels for recycling, says it has received the first US permit to purchase carriers sanctioned over Iran.

item.title: Thailand’s Trade Deficit Hits Record $10 Billion as Imports Soar
item.description: Higher imports of capital goods and raw materials, along with soaring oil and gas prices, pushed Thailand’s trade deficit to its widest level on record dating back to 1991.

item.title: Emerging-Market Assets Rise as Oil Drops on US-Iran Deal Hopes
item.description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose as oil prices declined on signs that the US and Iran were nearing a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Japan Bonds Face Homegrown Downside Risks Even as Oil Retreats
item.description: A gauge of risk compensation for holding government bonds has risen fastest in Japan among major markets since the US-Iran war began, pointing to local factors that may continue to weigh on Japanese bonds even if energy prices retreat.

item.title: AI Rally Drives Best Run in Global Momentum Stocks on Record
item.description: The AI boom is giving momentum investors their best return in decades, as the world’s hottest stocks power ahead despite worries over potentially slower growth due to the Iran war.

item.title: Sri Lanka Rupee to Recover From Plunge as Oil to Ease, BMI Says
item.description: The Sri Lankan rupee is set to recover from its slide by the end of this year as oil prices are set to decline and as the central bank raises interest rates, according to BMI.

item.title: Bank SMBC Indonesia Sells $1.1 Billion in Pension Loans to BTN
item.description: PT Bank SMBC Indonesia, a unit of Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp, is selling 19.9 trillion rupiah ($1.1 billion) worth of pension loans as it tilts away from its legacy business.

item.title: India Rupee Climbs on Oil Relief, Central Bank Valuation Comment
item.description: India’s rupee strengthened as oil prices dropped on hopes of a US-Iran deal and on comments by the central bank Governor that the currency may be undervalued.

item.title: FTSE Cut Hits Stock Tied to One of Indonesia’s Richest Families
item.description: Shares linked to one of Indonesia’s richest families dropped after FTSE Russell over the weekend removed the company from its global indexes due to concentrated shareholding.

item.title: Apple Watchに変革を、Whoopやオーラ台頭でヘルスケアアプリに課題－Power On
item.description: 健康ウエアラブル端末市場で競争激化、AI時代への対応課題に

item.title: Israel Set to Cut Rates as Efforts to End to Iran War Advance
item.description: Israel’s central bank is set to cut interest rates as the US and Iran appear to be edging closer to a deal to end the war which started in late February and has been in a tenuous ceasefire since early April.

item.title: China Trading Curbs May Hit $32 Billion of HK Assets, Citic Says
item.description: China’s latest crackdown on cross-border stock trading aimed at tightening control over capital outflows may affect as much as HK$250 billion ($32 billion) of assets in Hong Kong, according to Citic Securities.

item.title: India State Lenders Wilt As Iran War Lifts Yields to 2-Year High
item.description: A Nifty gauge of government-controlled banks has fallen 6% this month.

item.title: Sakura Internet Eyes More Spending to Meet AI Data Center Demand
item.description: Sakura Internet Inc.’s chief said the company may need to hike its capital spending by nearly seven times its initial plan to keep up with artificial intelligence demand in Japan.

item.title: India Raises Diesel, Gasoline Prices for Fourth Time This Month
item.description: India’s state-run fuel retailers raised gasoline and diesel prices for the fourth time this month as surging consumption during the agricultural harvest season and widening price disparities with private outlets strained inventories.

item.title: India Rupee May Be Undervalued, RBI Governor Tells Mint
item.description: The Indian rupee may now be undervalued following its recent depreciation, Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra said in an interview with Mint.

item.title: India Bond Investors Tap Soaring Swap Rates to Juice Returns
item.description: Multi-year high swap rates are driving some Indian debt fund managers into trades aimed at boosting fixed-income returns.

item.title: Huawei Touts Chipmaking Breakthrough to Shorten Gap With TSMC
item.description: Huawei Technologies Co. said it has come up with a new pathway to shorten its gap with industry leader Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., potentially achieving a breakthrough in making advanced semiconductors without cutting-edge equipment.

item.title: Chinese Mine Disaster Sparks Jump in Coal Prices and Stocks
item.description: Chinese coking coal futures jumped by the daily limit and mining stocks surged after a deadly accident in Shanxi province sparked fears of broader supply disruptions.

item.title: Chinese Firms Speed Up Plans to Build New Coal Power Plants: GEM
item.description: Chinese firms are accelerating the pace at which they propose new coal-fired power plants, even as the government moves to rein in growth after the rapid expansion in recent years.

item.title: Singapore Warns Economic Outlook Dims Despite First Quarter Beat
item.description: Singapore said its economic outlook for this year has weakened as the energy crunch from the Middle East conflict weighs on global growth, flagging macro risks despite a robust first quarter thanks to the AI boom.

item.title: Japan Bond Yield Surge Deepens Regional Bank Stock Divide
item.description: Japan’s rising bond yields are likely to widen the stock performance gap between regional lenders with weaker investment portfolios and those with stronger holdings, according to analysts.

item.title: Indonesia Eyes Commodity Export Policy Detail Rollout in Coming Weeks
item.description: Global markets and major trading partners can expect updates on Indonesia’s new commodity export policy within weeks as the government works on the legal and structural rollout of its new centralized export agency, according to a trade ministry official.

item.title: Gold Gains as Prospects of Iran Deal Temper Inflation Concerns
item.description: Gold rose as signs that the US and Iran were closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz tempered inflation concerns.

item.title: European Gas Declines on Optimism US and Iran Will Reach Deal
item.description: European natural gas dropped during early trading in Asia on optimism that the US and Iran are closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil, Dollar Slide on Optimism Over US-Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
item.description: Oil and the dollar fell while US stock futures climbed as risk appetite improved on hopes that a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore crude flows could be close.

item.title: Crude Oil Drops as US Inches Toward Iran Deal to Reopen Strait
item.description: Oil dropped at the week’s open as the US and Iran edged toward a deal, although President Donald Trump said that Washington’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would remain until an agreement was completed.
